Content of Message Bodies

Content of Message Bodies

Section titled “Content of Message Bodies”

The content field

Section titled “The content field”

This field can be found both in Response Objects and Request Body Objects.

This allows returning / accepting content in different formats , each one with a different structure described by the Media Type Object.

note

Wildcards are accepted for the media types, with the more specific ones taking precedence over the generic ones.

Media Type Object

Section titled “Media Type Object”

The Media Type Object describes the structure of the content and provides examples for documentation and mocking purposes.

The structure is described in the schema field

Schema Object

Section titled “Schema Object”

The Schema Object defines a data type which can be a primitive (integer, string, …), an array or an object depending on its type field.

type is a string(YAML format) and its possible values are:

  • number
  • string
  • boolean
  • array
  • object

Depending on the selected type, a number of other fields are available to further specify the data format.

Example integer with limited range:

Example string with only three valid options:

Array type

Section titled “Array type”

Array types must have an items field, which is a Schema Object itself.

note

//\\ All items inside array have to be the same type.

It means you can only define a single items’s type per array.

info

The size of the array can be limited with minItems and maxItems.

Object type

Section titled “Object type”

Object types must have a properties field listing the properties of the object.

This field is a key-value pairing property names (as a key) with a Schema Object(as a value).

Here’s an example defining an object with two fields: a productName string and a productPrice number:

Example

Section titled “ Example ”

From Tic Tac Toe sample API

The response contains an object is JSON format with two fields:

  • winner is a string with only three possible values: ., X and O.
  • board is a 3-element array where each item is another 3-element array, effectively building a 3x3 square matrix. Each element in the matrix is a string with only three possible values: ., X and O.

Sources

Section titled “Sources”
  • https://oai.github.io/Documentation/specification-content.html
